step_by_step_reasoning: We identify five main components from the whiteboard: 1) EC2 VMs for initial ad hoc data ingestion of genomics data (ATCGs); 2) Amazon FSx where raw ingested data is written; 3) Amazon ECR hosting frozen pipeline container versions; 4) Amazon EKS running the curation pipelines by mounting FSx and pulling from ECR; 5) An S3-based Data Lake where processed matrices are written. Finally, we map the stick figure to a UserCompanyAnalyst representing the downstream biologists and data scientists.

graph.name: Biopharma Genomic Data Curation Pipeline
graph.notes: Ingests raw genomics data using EC2, curates it at scale on EKS using versioned containers from ECR, and writes outputs to an S3 data lake.

nodes.name: Ad hoc EC2 VMs
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Used for ad hoc data ingestion of raw genomics data.

nodes.name: Amazon FSx
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores raw genomic sequences; mounted to EKS.

nodes.name: Amazon ECR
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Stores versioned Docker containers for pipelines.

nodes.name: Amazon EKS
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Scales to 100+ nodes to process genomic data.

nodes.name: Data Lake
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores output matrices for analytical consumption.

nodes.name: Data Scientists & Biologists
nodes.notes: Analyze final matrices to discover gene targets.

edges.notes: Ingested data is saved to FSx.
EKS pulls pipeline images from ECR.
EKS mounts FSx to access genomic sequences.
Processed matrices are saved to S3.
